paragraphs.en: The audio indicates that someone had called in from Baltimore and was on the way to that home at that time. The dispatcher said the caller believed that the door to the home was unlocked, but when emergency responders got there, they said it was deadbolted. That is according to the audio of the emergency dispatch on Broadcastify. The audio shows that about 25 minutes later they reported that CPR was in progress. We can play for you about a 10-second clip that gives some additional detail.
paragraphs.cn: Rekaman menunjukkan bahwa seseorang menelepon dari Baltimore, sedang dalam perjalanan ke rumah tersebut. Petugas operator mengatakan bahwa penelepon mengira pintu rumah terbuka, tetapi ketika petugas tiba, mereka mengatakan pintu terkunci. Ini berdasarkan rekaman darurat dari Broadcastify. Rekaman menunjukkan bahwa sekitar 25 menit kemudian, mereka melaporkan sedang melakukan CPR. Kami dapat memutar klip sekitar 10 detik untuk memberikan lebih banyak detail.

paragraphs.speaker: Brian Todd
paragraphs.en: This is from the emergency response broadcast on Broadcastify. Take a listen to this.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah siaran respons darurat dari Broadcastify. Silakan dengarkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: EMS six responds to engine seven medic seven on scene cardiac arrest. EMS six responds to engine seven medic seven with cardiac arrest.
paragraphs.cn: EMS Six Response Engine Seven Medic Seven, cardiac arrest di lokasi. EMS Six Response Engine Seven Medic Seven, cardiac arrest.

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: And, again, we can just, you know, again reiterate that that call discusses cardiac arrest. We cannot confirm at this hour that Senator Graham actually died of cardiac arrest. That was a call discussing cardiac arrest, piecing it together. You can also -- we can also tell you that emergency responders were dispatched to the address for someone -- for Senator Graham at about 8:30 p.m. Saturday for a report of someone suffering from chest pains.
paragraphs.cn: Sekali lagi, kami tegaskan bahwa panggilan itu membahas tentang cardiac arrest. Saat ini kami tidak dapat memastikan apakah Senator Graham benar-benar meninggal karena cardiac arrest. Itu adalah panggilan yang membahas cardiac arrest, dan kami sedang menyusun informasi. Kami juga dapat memberi tahu Anda bahwa sekitar pukul 8:30 malam pada hari Sabtu, petugas darurat dikirim ke alamat tersebut untuk menangani laporan nyeri dada untuk Senator Graham.

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: That is according to audio of the emergency dispatch on the service Broadcastify. The audio, again, indicates that someone had called in from Baltimore and was on the way to the home at that time at about 8:30 p.m. on Saturday. The dispatcher said the caller believed the door was unlocked, but emergency responders said it was deadbolted. That is according to that audio. And about 25 minutes later they reported that CPR was in progress.
paragraphs.cn: Ini berdasarkan rekaman darurat dari layanan Broadcastify. Rekaman kembali menunjukkan bahwa seseorang menelepon dari Baltimore sekitar pukul 8:30 malam pada hari Sabtu, sedang dalam perjalanan ke kediaman tersebut. Petugas operator mengatakan bahwa penelepon mengira pintu tidak terkunci, tetapi petugas darurat mengatakan pintu terkunci. Ini berdasarkan rekaman itu. Sekitar 25 menit kemudian, mereka melaporkan sedang melakukan CPR.

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: We can also tell you that the governor of South Carolina, Henry McMaster, is empowered to appoint a temporary replacement for Senator Graham. We believe that Senator -- that Governor McMaster is, of course, dealing with that situation right now. Governor McMaster issued a statement posted on X saying, quote, "Peggy and I and our children are devastated. Lindsey Graham is irreplaceable, the fiercest of fighters for South Carolina and America, and a loyal and steadfast friend.
paragraphs.cn: Kami juga dapat memberi tahu Anda bahwa Gubernur South Carolina Henry McMaster memiliki wewenang untuk menunjuk pengganti sementara Senator Graham. Kami yakin Gubernur McMaster tentu sedang menangani situasi ini. Gubernur McMaster dalam pernyataan di X mengatakan: "Peggy, saya, dan anak-anak kami sangat berduka. Lindsey Graham tidak tergantikan, dia adalah pejuang terkuat South Carolina dan Amerika, serta teman yang setia dan teguh."

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: We grieve with Darline, his family, and his devoted staff. May God hold him gently in the palm of his hand. We shall not see his likes again." That is a statement from Governor Henry McMaster of South Carolina. Governor McMaster is empowered to appoint a temporary replacement for Senator Graham. That reference to Darline is a reference to Graham's younger sister, Darline. Rene?
paragraphs.cn: "Kami berduka bersama Darlene, keluarganya, dan staf setianya. Semoga Tuhan memeluknya dengan lembut. Kita tidak akan pernah melihat orang seperti dia lagi." Itu adalah pernyataan Gubernur South Carolina Henry McMaster. Gubernur McMaster memiliki wewenang untuk menunjuk pengganti sementara Senator Graham. Darlene yang disebut dalam pernyataan itu adalah saudara perempuan Graham, Darlene. Rene?

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Brian Todd, thank you. And for more, we are joined by CNN Presidential Historian Tim Naftali. He's a former director of the Nixon Presidential Library. Thanks so much for joining us this morning on this breaking news day, really shocking news surrounding the death of Senator Lindsey Graham. I want, from your perspective, when historians look back at Graham's life and his career, what do you think will be that defining chapter of his public life?
paragraphs.cn: Brian Todd, terima kasih. Selanjutnya, kami bersama sejarawan presiden CNN Tim Naftali. Dia pernah menjadi direktur Perpustakaan Kepresidenan Nixon. Terima kasih banyak telah bergabung dengan kami pagi ini, di hari berita mendadak ini, berita tentang meninggalnya Senator Lindsey Graham sungguh mengejutkan. Saya ingin melihat dari sudut pandang Anda, ketika sejarawan melihat kembali kehidupan dan karier Graham, menurut Anda apa babak paling menentukan dalam kehidupan publiknya?

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: Well, I think there have been a few chapters of Senator Graham's life that will define his long public career. When he was in the House at the very beginning of his congressional career, he was a House manager for the impeachment of Bill Clinton. And at that time, even though he was a new member of the House, he gained national prominence in making certain arguments about impeachment and why a crime wasn't necessary to remove a president. It depended on their character and whether they brought honor to the office.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ada beberapa babak dalam kehidupan Senator Graham yang akan mendefinisikan karier publiknya yang panjang. Di awal karier kongresnya sebagai anggota DPR, dia adalah manajer DPR dalam pemakzulan Bill Clinton. Saat itu, meskipun dia anggota baru DPR, dia mendapatkan ketenaran nasional karena mengajukan argumen tertentu dalam masalah pemakzulan, yaitu bahwa pemecatan presiden tidak harus memerlukan kejahatan, tetapi tergantung pada karakter mereka dan apakah mereka membawa kehormatan pada jabatan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: When he replaced Strom Thurmond in the Senate, Lindsey Graham gained in national recognition as Republicans gained control of the Senate. Lindsey Graham was famous for reaching across the aisle. He worked with Senator Ted Kennedy on immigration reform. He was known to -- he spoke of the importance of bipartisanship, although he was very clear about his own conservative values. In foreign policy, he was an advocate of a strong American presence and a strong American involvement in international affairs.
paragraphs.cn: Ketika dia menggantikan Strom Thurmond di Senat, dengan Partai Republik menguasai Senat, Lindsey Graham mendapatkan pengakuan nasional. Lindsey Graham dikenal karena melintasi batas partai. Dia bekerja sama dengan Senator Ted Kennedy dalam reformasi imigrasi. Dia dikenal karena menekankan pentingnya kerja sama bipartisan, meskipun dia sangat jelas tentang nilai-nilai konservatifnya. Dalam kebijakan luar negeri, dia menganjurkan kehadiran dan keterlibatan aktif Amerika dalam urusan internasional.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: He was viewed as a neoconservative in the George W. Bush era. He was a strong advocate for Israel, but also a strong advocate for NATO and in most recent years for Ukraine. He was in 2008, but well before then, a very close ally of Republican nominee, Senator John McCain. Indeed, they were considered very close friends. And so he was associated with Senator McCain's views of foreign policy and also with Senator McCain's willingness to work with Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: Di era George W. Bush, dia dipandang sebagai neokonservatif. Dia adalah pendukung setia Israel, pendukung setia NATO, dan dalam beberapa tahun terakhir, pendukung setia Ukraina. Dia adalah sekutu dekat Senator John McCain, calon presiden dari Partai Republik pada tahun 2008, bahkan sebelumnya. Faktanya, mereka dianggap sebagai teman dekat. Oleh karena itu, dia terkait dengan pandangan kebijakan luar negeri Senator McCain dan kesediaan Senator McCain untuk bekerja sama dengan Demokrat.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: The next phase is the phase, of course, that is most recent, which is where Senator Graham became close to Donald Trump, President Trump. And at times seemed to be contradicting, if only in spirit, but perhaps in act, some of the commitments that he had shown to the ideas of John McCain and also to the bipartisanship that had shaped some of his actions in the first couple of decades of his time in Washington.
paragraphs.cn: Babak berikutnya tentu saja adalah babak yang paling baru, yaitu hubungan dekat Senator Graham dengan Donald Trump, Presiden Trump. Kadang-kadang, dia tampaknya setidaknya secara spiritual, mungkin juga dalam tindakan, bertentangan dengan komitmen yang pernah dia tunjukkan terhadap ide-ide John McCain dan kerja sama bipartisan yang membentuk sebagian dari dua dekade pertamanya di Washington.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: In most recent years, despite the friendship, the clear friendship with Donald Trump, Senator Graham has continued to support NATO and has viewed the plight of Ukrainians as something that is a concern of American international security. And he has viewed -- and continued to view Russia as a threat. So when looking at his career, one sees a man with conservative values who represents in the big transformation, political transformation of the South.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam beberapa tahun terakhir, meskipun memiliki persahabatan yang jelas dengan Donald Trump, Senator Graham terus mendukung NATO dan menganggap penderitaan rakyat Ukraina sebagai bagian dari kepentingan keamanan internasional Amerika. Dia selalu menganggap Rusia sebagai ancaman. Jadi, sepanjang kariernya, dia adalah seorang konservatif yang mewakili transformasi politik besar di Selatan.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: Lindsey Graham replaced a Democrat when he was elected in the -- to the House from South Carolina and replaced Strom Thurmond. Strom Thurmond had started his career as a Democrat running against Harry Truman for the nomination of the Democratic Party in 1948. He switched to the Republican Party, but Lindsey Graham had been elected as a Republican. So Graham's career indicated was part of the shift to a solid South or somewhat solid South now for the Republican Party.
paragraphs.cn: Lindsey Graham menggantikan seorang Demokrat ketika terpilih sebagai anggota DPR dari South Carolina, dan menggantikan Strom Thurmond. Karier politik Thurmond dimulai sebagai Demokrat, bersaing dengan Harry Truman untuk nominasi presiden dari Partai Demokrat pada tahun 1948. Dia kemudian pindah ke Partai Republik, tetapi Graham terpilih sebagai anggota Partai Republik. Oleh karena itu, karier Graham menandai peralihan Selatan ke Partai Republik, atau dengan kata lain, Selatan kini menjadi basis kuat Partai Republik.

paragraphs.speaker: Tim Naftali
paragraphs.en: And though he showed a desire for bipartisanship throughout most of his career, in recent years, he has seemed more partisan as he got closer to Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: Meskipun dia menunjukkan keinginan untuk kerja sama bipartisan selama sebagian besar kariernya, dalam beberapa tahun terakhir, dengan hubungan yang semakin erat dengan Donald Trump, dia tampak lebih partisan.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes. And his evolution on all of the things that you laid out certainly is a part of his legacy as well. Tim Naftali, thank you so much for joining us this morning. And we have more breaking news coverage on the death of Senator Lindsey Graham. Coming up, Graham's work on the world stage and the tributes pouring in from international leaders after the break.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Evolusinya dalam semua masalah yang Anda sebutkan tidak diragukan lagi juga merupakan bagian dari warisan politiknya. Tim Naftali, terima kasih banyak telah berpartisipasi dalam acara kami pagi ini. Selanjutnya kami akan melanjutkan liputan berita mendadak tentang meninggalnya Senator Lindsey Graham. Nantikan laporan tentang kerja Graham di panggung internasional dan ucapan belasungkawa dari para pemimpin internasional.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Well, this morning we are seeing reaction to the sudden death of South Carolina Republican Senator Lindsey Graham. Graham was known for his advocacy and strong support for Israel. And just hours after news broke of his death, several Israeli leaders have expressed their sadness at his passing. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu posted this in part on social media, quote, "Israel has lost one of its greatest friends. America lost a great patriot.
paragraphs.cn: Pagi ini, kami melihat reaksi atas meninggalnya Senator Partai Republik dari South Carolina Lindsey Graham secara mendadak. Graham dikenal karena advokasi dan dukungan kuatnya terhadap Israel. Hanya beberapa jam setelah berita kematiannya, beberapa pemimpin Israel menyampaikan belasungkawa. Perdana Menteri Israel Benjamin Netanyahu di media sosial mengatakan: "Israel kehilangan salah satu teman terbesarnya. Amerika kehilangan seorang patriot besar. Saya kehilangan seorang sahabat."

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: I have lost a beloved friend." Well, for reaction in Israel, we turn now to Jerusalem Bureau Chief Oren Liebermann. Oren, 2 o'clock in the afternoon there. What are you hearing from people as far as reaction to the shocking news?
paragraphs.cn: Selanjutnya, kami menghubungi kepala biro Yerusalem Oren Liebermann untuk mengetahui reaksi dari pihak Israel. Oren, di sana jam dua siang, bagaimana reaksi orang-orang terhadap berita mengejutkan ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: Rene, Israel sees the passing of Senator Lindsey Graham as a huge loss. He was a passionate advocate for Israel, a staunch supporter of Israel. And he was hawkish on Iran as well as Israel's other adversaries in the region. And that made him Israel's great ally in Washington. He would advocate for Israel to have more funding when it came to missile defense. He was a big proponent of that.
paragraphs.cn: Rene, Israel menganggap meninggalnya Senator Lindsey Graham sebagai kerugian besar. Dia adalah advokat yang bersemangat dan pendukung setia Israel. Sikap kerasnya terhadap Iran dan lawan-lawan Israel lainnya di kawasan menjadikannya sekutu penting Israel di Washington. Dia menganjurkan lebih banyak dana untuk pertahanan rudal Israel dan sangat mendukung hal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: And then he visited often, and not only since October 7, 2023, for decades here, likely going back to when he became a senator in 2002. He would visit Israel. He would hold press conferences here. He would meet not only the media but also officials. Of course, he would meet Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and others repeatedly. He made it a point to be very openly and publicly pro-Israel.
paragraphs.cn: Dia sering mengunjungi Israel, tidak hanya sejak 7 Oktober 2023, tetapi selama puluhan tahun, mungkin sejak dia menjadi senator pada tahun 2002. Dia akan mengunjungi Israel, mengadakan konferensi pers di sini, tidak hanya bertemu media tetapi juga pejabat. Tentu saja, dia akan berulang kali bertemu dengan Perdana Menteri Benjamin Netanyahu dan lainnya. Dia secara terbuka dan jelas mendukung Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: And that's why Israeli officials, not just from the coalition, not just from Netanyahu's side, but also from the opposition, view his passing as a huge loss. You saw some of the statement there from Netanyahu. Let me read you just a couple of other statements. Former Prime Minister Naftali Bennett and one of Netanyahu's key challengers in the upcoming election said, quote, "I am heartbroken to hear of the passing of my friend Senator Lindsey Graham.
paragraphs.cn: Itulah mengapa para pejabat Israel -- -- tidak hanya dari koalisi pemerintahan dan kubu Netanyahu, tetapi juga dari oposisi -- -- menganggap kematiannya sebagai kerugian besar. Anda melihat sebagian pernyataan Netanyahu. Biarkan saya membaca beberapa pernyataan lainnya. Mantan Perdana Menteri Naftali Bennett -- -- salah satu penantang utama Netanyahu dalam pemilihan mendatang -- -- mengatakan: "Mendengar teman saya Senator Lindsey Graham meninggal, hati saya hancur. Amerika kehilangan seorang patriot yang setia, Israel kehilangan salah satu teman terbesarnya."

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: America has lost a devoted patriot, and Israel has lost one of its greatest friends." Former Defense Minister Avigdor Lieberman said in a statement, "Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, one of the greatest supporters of the state of Israel, has passed away.
paragraphs.cn: 前国防部长阿维格多·利伯曼在一份声明中说："共和党参议员林赛·格雷厄姆，以色列国最伟大的支持者之一，已经去世。"

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: For years he stood by our side, even when it required courage and even when it involved significant criticism." And then former Prime Minister Yair Lapid said, "Lindsey had unwavering moral clarity, a deep devotion of the people of Israel and a clear vision for a secure and peaceful Middle East." Even when there was a break between Netanyahu and President Donald Trump, some sort of division on how to handle a regional issue, Graham had Israel's ear, and he would be able to go to Trump and advocate Israel's
paragraphs.cn: 多年来，他一直站在我们身边，即使这需要勇气，即使这招致了严厉批评。" 随后，前总理亚伊尔·拉皮德表示："林赛有着坚定不移的道德清晰度、对以色列人民的深厚奉献，以及对一个安全和平的中东的明确愿景。" 即使在内塔尼亚胡与唐纳德·特朗普总统之间出现分歧，即在如何处理地区问题上存在某种分裂时，格雷厄姆仍能倾听以色列的意见，并且他能够去找特朗普，为以色列的立场辩护，或至少试图在这些问题上影响特朗普的想法。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: position or at least try to sway Trump's thinking when it came to some of these issues. And, of course, crucially, especially at this time, that was on Iran. He openly expressed skepticism that Iran was negotiating in good intent and that it would stick to the terms of the Memorandum of Understanding or that that 60-day negotiation window would ever lead to a final agreement. Israel, and specifically Netanyahu, have had those same doubts about Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，关键的是，尤其是在这个时候，那就是关于伊朗的问题。他公开表示怀疑伊朗是否在真诚谈判，是否会遵守谅解备忘录的条款，或者那60天的谈判窗口是否会最终达成协议。以色列，特别是内塔尼亚胡，对伊朗也有同样的疑虑。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: And for Israel, it was frankly critical to have somebody in Washington, to have Graham, who was so close to Trump, advocating caution on the MOU, on Iran, and frankly advocating for, if necessary, a return to strikes, a return to the joint U.S.- Israeli military campaign. And that's just one of the many reasons that Israel viewed Graham as such an important ally in Washington. And at this point, it's unclear who will take that place.
paragraphs.cn: 对以色列来说，坦率地说，在华盛顿有像格雷厄姆这样与特朗普关系密切的人，在谅解备忘录和伊朗问题上倡导谨慎，并在必要时主张恢复打击、恢复美以联合军事行动，是至关重要的。而这只是以色列将格雷厄姆视为华盛顿重要盟友的众多原因之一。目前，尚不清楚谁将接替他的位置。

paragraphs.speaker: Oren Liebermann
paragraphs.en: According to an Israeli source, Netanyahu is considering going to the funeral of Senator Lindsey Graham, whenever that's scheduled here in the coming days. That, too, may also give him a chance to meet Trump. The two, in their last known phone call or publicly acknowledged phone call, had talked about meeting in the near future, although a date hadn't been scheduled yet, so perhaps that happens around the funeral of Senator Lindsey Graham. Rene?
paragraphs.cn: 据一位以色列消息人士称，内塔尼亚胡正在考虑参加参议员林赛·格雷厄姆的葬礼，无论葬礼在未来几天何时举行。这也可能让他有机会与特朗普会面。两人在最近一次已知或公开承认的通话中，曾讨论过在不久的将来会面，尽管尚未确定具体日期，因此可能会在参议员林赛·格雷厄姆的葬礼前后进行。蕾妮？

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Oren, thank you. And Senator Graham's final foreign trip was actually to Ukraine, another place that he was a fierce advocate for Ukraine. And on Friday, he met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. And in a social media post, Zelenskyy commented on Graham's relentless diplomatic work to support Ukraine in its battle against Russia. Now, CNN's International Diplomatic Editor Nic Robertson, he joins us now with more on this part of Graham's legacy. Nic?
paragraphs.cn: 奥伦，谢谢。格雷厄姆参议员的最后一次外访实际上是去乌克兰，这是另一个他强烈支持的地方。周五，他会见了乌克兰总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基。泽连斯基在社交媒体上评论了格雷厄姆为支持乌克兰对抗俄罗斯而进行的不懈外交工作。现在，CNN国际外交编辑尼克·罗伯逊将加入我们，带来更多关于格雷厄姆这一部分遗产的报道。尼克？

paragraphs.speaker: Nic Robertson
paragraphs.en: Yes, and it's a legacy that's so important, not just to Ukraine, but to Ukraine's European partners, who were so concerned about President Trump's sort of MAGA-based, more isolationist vision of the United States' future on the global stage, on foreign policy issues like Ukraine, like confronting President Putin. That they saw Lindsey Graham as somebody that was a kindred spirit, the sort of old-school republicanism on foreign policy, as you will, willing to intervene.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这份遗产非常重要，不仅对乌克兰，而且对乌克兰的欧洲伙伴也是如此。他们非常担心特朗普总统基于MAGA的、更加孤立主义的美国在全球舞台上的未来愿景，尤其是在乌克兰和对抗普京总统等外交政策问题上。他们认为林赛·格雷厄姆是一个志同道合的人，是那种在外交政策上持老派共和党立场、愿意干预的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Nic Robertson
paragraphs.en: Maybe they didn't back him on all the interventions like Iran and things like that. But when it came to Ukraine, you get a sense of that from what we've heard from the NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte, mourning the passing of Lindsey Graham, but saying that he was, you know, strongly believed in the NATO alliance and that he was working on trying to bring an end to the war. And that is something that we've heard from President Zelenskyy.
paragraphs.cn: 也许他们并不支持他在伊朗等问题上的所有干预。但谈到乌克兰，我们从北约秘书长马克·吕特的发言中能感受到这一点。吕特哀悼林赛·格雷厄姆的去世，但表示他坚信北约联盟，并致力于努力结束战争。这也是我们从泽连斯基总统那里听到的。

paragraphs.speaker: Nic Robertson
paragraphs.en: You know, President Zelenskyy has talked about the 10 times that the senator visited, Lindsey Graham visited Ukraine during Russia's war there since 2022. So a phenomenal high number of visits. And remembering, these visits are not easy. You're going into an active war zone. You're going into Kyiv, a city that is under active and almost daily attack by Russia. It's not somewhere you can fly into. So the transport, the getting there is difficult and takes a long time. So not an easy thing to do.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，泽连斯基总统曾提到，自2022年俄罗斯发动战争以来，这位参议员访问了乌克兰10次。这是一个非常高的访问次数。而且要知道，这些访问并不容易。你要进入一个活跃的战区，进入基辅，一个几乎每天遭受俄罗斯攻击的城市。那里不能乘飞机进入。所以交通、到达那里都很困难，需要很长时间。这不是一件容易的事。

paragraphs.speaker: Nic Robertson
paragraphs.en: And that, to the Ukrainians, was hugely important. President Zelenskyy saying that Senator Graham was there whenever the country needed him. And they needed him because he could communicate, as Oren was saying, for Netanyahu in the White House, that he could communicate with President Trump. And that was the value to the Europeans as well. Go back to remembering that moment when President Trump had the horrible falling out of President Zelenskyy early last year.
paragraphs.cn: 这对乌克兰人来说非常重要。泽连斯基总统表示，每当国家需要他时，格雷厄姆参议员都在那里。他们需要他，因为他能够像奥伦所说的那样，为内塔尼亚胡在白宫沟通，能够与特朗普总统沟通。这对欧洲人来说也是价值所在。回想一下去年初特朗普总统与泽连斯基总统发生严重冲突的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Nic Robertson
paragraphs.en: The Europeans responded by forming the Coalition of the Willing. It was Lindsey Graham that was a U.S. representative on that Coalition of the Willing because he understood the importance of standing with Ukraine. So on this latest trip, he spoke about his latest efforts to try to get an end to the war. This is what he said. SEN. LINDSEY GRAHAM (R),
paragraphs.cn: 欧洲人的回应是组建了意愿联盟。林赛·格雷厄姆是该联盟中的美国代表，因为他理解支持乌克兰的重要性。因此，在最近这次访问中，他谈到了他为结束战争所做的最新努力。以下是他所说的话。参议员林赛·格雷厄姆（共和党）：

paragraphs.speaker: South Carolina
paragraphs.en: We've reached agreement with the White House on a version of the Russian sanctions bill that they will support. It means it's going to become law. So when I get back to Washington, I'm going to go with Senator Blumenthal, to the Republican and Democratic leader to see if we can find time to move this Russian sanctions package that would give tools to President Trump to help end this war.
paragraphs.cn: 我们已与白宫就他们支持的俄罗斯制裁法案版本达成一致。这意味着它将成为法律。所以当我回到华盛顿后，我将与参议员布卢门撒尔一起去找共和党和民主党领袖，看看能否找到时间推进这项俄罗斯制裁方案，该方案将为特朗普总统提供工具，以帮助结束这场战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Robertson
paragraphs.en: And ending the war is exactly what the European partners wanted. And of course, Europeans also have been pressuring President Trump to put more sanctions on Russia to bring the economic impact on Russia. Another kind of important point that Lindsey Graham made during that visit to Kyiv was pointing out that China's support for President Putin was key and vital to keeping the war going. He was all about putting more pressure on China to end that support as well.
paragraphs.cn: 结束战争正是欧洲伙伴们所期望的。当然，欧洲人也一直在向特朗普总统施压，要求对俄罗斯实施更多制裁，以对俄罗斯经济造成影响。林赛·格雷厄姆在访问基辅期间提出的另一个重要观点是，指出中国对普京总统的支持是战争持续的关键和重要因素。他主张向中国施加更大压力，以结束这种支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Robertson
paragraphs.en: So he got the big global picture in the same way that the United States' international allies did that oftentimes it was perceived President Trump didn't.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，他与美国的国际盟友一样，看到了全局，而人们常常认为特朗普总统没有看到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Nic Robertson, thank you so much for that perspective. And stick with CNN, we'll have more on our breaking news on the coverage of the death of U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham right after this break. Reaction from Capitol Hill where he was a strong voice for President Trump's agenda.
paragraphs.cn: 尼克·罗伯逊，非常感谢你的分析。请继续关注CNN，我们将在广告后带来更多关于美国参议员林赛·格雷厄姆去世的突发新闻。来自国会山的反应，他是特朗普总统议程的有力支持者。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: We are continuing to follow the breaking news out of Washington. Longtime Republican Senator Lindsey Graham has died from a, quote, "brief and sudden illness." His office announced this earlier this morning. Graham served as a senator for South Carolina for more than two decades. Initial 911 audio revealed emergency personnel respond to a call for chest pains at Graham's home in D.C. around 8:30 in the evening. EMS reported that CPR was in progress about 25 minutes later.
paragraphs.cn: 我们继续关注来自华盛顿的突发新闻。长期担任共和党参议员的林赛·格雷厄姆因"短暂突发疾病"去世。他的办公室今早宣布了这一消息。格雷厄姆担任南卡罗来纳州参议员超过二十年。最初的911录音显示，紧急救援人员于晚上8点30分左右接到格雷厄姆在华盛顿家中胸痛的求助电话。急救人员报告称，大约25分钟后正在进行心肺复苏。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Senator Graham had a brief run for the Republican presidential nomination in 2015 when he was a critic of Donald Trump. But that changed after Trump took office in 2017. Graham quickly became a vocal ally. By Trump's second term, Graham became one of Trump's most trusted voices in the Senate. And Senator Graham was also known as a foreign policy hawk. He fiercely defended U.S. support for Israel and Ukraine. In fact, he had just returned from Kyiv where he met with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy on Friday.
paragraphs.cn: 格雷厄姆参议员在2015年曾短暂竞选共和党总统候选人提名，当时他是唐纳德·特朗普的批评者。但特朗普在2017年上任后，情况发生了变化。格雷厄姆迅速成为特朗普的直言不讳的盟友。到特朗普第二任期时，格雷厄姆已成为参议院中特朗普最信任的声音之一。格雷厄姆参议员还以外交政策鹰派著称。他坚决捍卫美国对以色列和乌克兰的支持。事实上，他刚从基辅返回，周五在那里会见了乌克兰总统泽连斯基。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Lindsey Graham turned 71 just last week. With us now is CNN Chief Congressional Correspondent Manu Raju. Manu, you know, you have been on Capitol Hill for decades and you have known Lindsey Graham for decades. From your perspective, what will be his lasting legacy?
paragraphs.cn: 林赛·格雷厄姆上周刚满71岁。现在连线的是CNN首席国会记者马努·拉朱。马努，你在国会山工作了几十年，也认识林赛·格雷厄姆几十年。从你的角度看，他留下的持久遗产是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: Well, it will be profound. He was a towering figure in the United States Senate and in Republican politics, an influential voice not just on foreign policy. Of course, he has been a longtime interventionist, and the interventionist wing of the Republican Party has not shifted away from that stance throughout his very long career, but also on the domestic front as well. He plays such a key role on so many issues and was the person that really drove the debate on Capitol Hill on some of those critical issues.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这将意义深远。他是美国参议院和共和党政治中的一位重要人物，不仅在对外政策上具有影响力。当然，他长期以来一直是干预主义者，共和党内的干预主义派别在他漫长的职业生涯中从未偏离这一立场，而且在国内事务上也是如此。他在许多问题上都扮演着关键角色，并且是在国会山推动一些关键问题辩论的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: I mean, just even back when he was a member of the House, he was one of the impeachment managers for the Clinton impeachment trial, the Bill Clinton impeachment trial. That really put him on the map nationally. Then ultimately, he ran for the United States Senate, won the United States Senate seat. He was a longtime ally of the late Senator John McCain, as well as the late Senator Joe Lieberman. They were referred to as the three amigos on Capitol Hill because they often broke ranks with their own party.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，早在他担任众议员时，他就是克林顿弹劾案（比尔·克林顿弹劾案）的弹劾经理之一。这让他真正在全国范围内声名鹊起。最终，他竞选美国参议员并赢得了参议院席位。他是已故参议员约翰·麦凯恩以及已故参议员乔·利伯曼的长期盟友。他们在国会山被称为"三剑客"，因为他们经常与自己的党派意见相左。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: They cut bipartisan deals. Lindsey Graham, a dealmaker on immigration, a supporter of comprehensive immigration reform, an issue that got him so much trouble from the right flank of his party, facing a Tea Party threat that threatened his career back in the 2014 primary. He ultimately fended that off by some shrewd and deft maneuvering, defending his bipartisan deal making, even on issues like climate change, things that were not within the GOP orthodoxy.
paragraphs.cn: 他们达成两党协议。林赛·格雷厄姆是移民问题上的交易撮合者，支持全面移民改革，这个问题让他在党内右翼中遇到很多麻烦，在2014年初选中面临茶党威胁，危及他的政治生涯。他最终通过精明而巧妙的策略化解了威胁，捍卫了他的两党合作，甚至在气候变化等问题上也是如此，而这些并不符合共和党的正统观念。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: And you mentioned him being a staunch critic of Donald Trump when he ran for president back in 2016. That presidential race did not go over for him. But he warned Republicans about backing Donald Trump, warning that it would destroy the party.
paragraphs.cn: 你提到他在2016年竞选总统时是唐纳德·特朗普的坚定批评者。那场总统竞选对他来说并不顺利。但他警告共和党人不要支持唐纳德·特朗普，警告说这会毁掉共和党。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: Ultimately, he decided that he made the wrong calculation there, siding with Donald Trump time and time again, aligning himself, becoming a frequent golf partner of Trump's, defending him on some of the more controversial issues throughout Trump's 10 years here in public life. Ultimately, something that his departure, people will remember in these later years of him being that stalwart ally of Donald Trump. But he did break with his party.
paragraphs.cn: 最终，他认定自己当初的判断有误，一次又一次地站在唐纳德·特朗普一边，与他结盟，成为特朗普频繁的高尔夫球友，并在特朗普公开生活的十年里，在一些更具争议的问题上为他辩护。最终，他的离世让人们记住的是他晚年作为唐纳德·特朗普坚定盟友的形象。但他确实与自己的政党决裂了。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: He warned his party time and time again that it was time to cut deals with Democrats. That happened during the Obama years as well. Remember, he voted for the two Supreme Court justices that Barack Obama put on the Supreme Court. But he also played a critical role in getting Brett Kavanaugh confirmed to the Supreme Court during those very contentious hearings on Capitol Hill, playing a role that really won him over with the right flank of his party.
paragraphs.cn: 他一次又一次地警告自己的政党，是时候与民主党达成协议了。这在奥巴马执政时期也是如此。记得吗，他投票支持了巴拉克·奥巴马提名的两位最高法院大法官。但他也在国会山那些充满争议的听证会上，为布雷特·卡瓦诺获得最高法院确认发挥了关键作用，这一角色确实为他赢得了党内右翼的支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Manu Raju
paragraphs.en: People had been skeptical of those bipartisan deals over the years. So, he leaves a very profound, complex legacy on the Hill, where he played such a mighty role on some of these key issues that really have affected Americans' lives and people around the world, given his role on the international front as well.
paragraphs.cn: Selama bertahun-tahun, orang-orang skeptis terhadap kesepakatan bipartisan ini. Oleh karena itu, ia meninggalkan warisan yang sangat dalam dan kompleks di Capitol Hill, ia memainkan peran besar dalam beberapa isu kunci yang benar-benar memengaruhi rakyat Amerika dan orang-orang di seluruh dunia, mengingat perannya di panggung internasional juga.

paragraphs.speaker: Rene Marsh
paragraphs.en: And, Manu, I mean, you really got to know him very well. I mean, tell us more. What was Senator Graham like on Capitol Hill during your interactions with him?
paragraphs.cn: Manu, maksudku, kamu benar-benar mengenalnya. Ceritakan lebih banyak kepada kami. Dalam interaksimu dengan Senator Graham, seperti apa dia di Capitol Hill?

paragraphs.speaker: Raju
paragraphs.en: He was very witty. He was very someone who was quick with a good quote. He didn't always want to talk to the press. Sometimes he was very clear. He did not want anything to do with talking to reporters. He was busy. He was oftentimes on his phone walking through the halls of the Capitol. But when he wanted to talk, he would let loose. He was not afraid to dive very into his views. If he had a controversial position on something, he would defend that controversial position.
paragraphs.cn: Dia sangat lucu. Dia cepat tanggap dan selalu bisa mengucapkan kata-kata yang brilian. Dia tidak selalu ingin berbicara dengan media. Terkadang dia sangat jelas, tidak ingin berinteraksi dengan wartawan sama sekali. Dia sibuk, sering berjalan sambil menelepon melewati koridor Capitol. Tapi ketika dia ingin bicara, dia akan berbicara terus terang. Dia tidak takut untuk menguraikan pandangannya. Jika dia memiliki posisi kontroversial dalam suatu hal, dia akan membela posisi kontroversial itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Raju
paragraphs.en: He was someone who reporters like myself would often stake out, as you can see there on your screen, because knowing that he had something to say about something very critical, he could help drive the debate on a number of issues. I mean, reporters would stake out a key position in the Senate basement, knowing that he was going to leave his office and walk to the Senate floor through that position that became known as the Russell basement stakeout location.
paragraphs.cn: Dia adalah target yang sering diincar wartawan seperti saya, seperti yang Anda lihat di layar, karena tahu dia punya sesuatu untuk dikatakan tentang hal-hal yang sangat penting, dia bisa mendorong perdebatan dalam banyak isu. Maksudku, wartawan akan menunggu di lokasi kunci di ruang bawah tanah Senat, tahu bahwa dia akan meninggalkan kantornya dan berjalan melewati tempat yang kemudian disebut 'Ruang Bawah Tanah Russell tempat mengintai' menuju ruang sidang Senat.

paragraphs.speaker: Raju
paragraphs.en: I started covering him there on Capitol Hill because he would go by there and stop and talk to reporters there. So, he was someone frequently to speak to, a towering figure, a person that a lot of people on Capitol Hill will miss. And you're hearing that outpouring of shock and concern and people remembering him at this moment after his sudden death overnight.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mulai meliputnya di Capitol Hill karena dia akan lewat sana, berhenti dan berbicara dengan wartawan. Jadi, dia adalah seorang pembicara yang sering tampil, seorang figur besar, dan banyak orang di Capitol Hill akan merindukannya. Setelah kematian mendadaknya tadi malam, Anda mendengar keterkejutan, kepedulian, dan kerinduan yang mengalir dari orang-orang.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Thank you, Manu. And I know you're going to have a lot more on this. Be sure to catch Manu at the top of the hour for "Inside Politics Sunday" for much more on the passing of Lindsey Graham. We're also watching the breaking news in the war with Iran as the U.S. targets Iranian military sites in their latest strikes.
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih, Manu. Aku tahu kamu akan melaporkan lebih banyak. Pastikan untuk menonton 'Inside Politics' Manu pada jam untuk mengetahui lebih banyak tentang kematian Lindsey Graham. Kami juga mengikuti berita terbaru tentang perang dengan Iran, AS menargetkan pangkalan militer Iran dalam serangan terbaru.

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN Breaking News.
paragraphs.cn: Ini CNN Breaking News.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Well, taking a live look at the traffic in the Strait of Hormuz right now. The Joint Maritime Information Center says it's open to two-way traffic. That's despite Iran's claim it is closed. But the security threat level in the waterway remains severe. And in recent hours, U.S. Central Command shared new video of strikes the U.S. military says it carried out on 140 Iranian military targets. The U.S. is blaming the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps for an attack on a merchant ship in the Strait.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang mari kita lihat lalu lintas di Selat Hormuz secara real-time. Pusat Informasi Maritim Gabungan mengatakan selat itu terbuka untuk lalu lintas dua arah, meskipun Iran mengklaim telah menutupnya. Namun tingkat ancaman keamanan di jalur air itu masih serius. Dalam beberapa jam terakhir, Komando Pusat AS merilis video baru yang menunjukkan serangan AS terhadap 140 target militer Iran. AS menuduh Garda Revolusi Islam menyerang sebuah kapal dagang di selat tersebut.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: CNN's Nada Bashir joins me now. Nada, what are we hearing or learning about the latest with these strikes this morning?
paragraphs.cn: Nada Bashir dari CNN bergabung dengan kami sekarang. Nada, apa yang kami dengar atau ketahui tentang perkembangan terbaru serangan pagi ini?

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: Well, this is the third round of U.S. strikes targeting Iran that we have seen in recent days. And this comes, of course, after the Iranian regime confirmed that it would be shutting the Strait of Hormuz down once again to the key shipping access route. And, of course, we've been learning more from the U.S. military as well. We've heard from the U.S. Central Command saying that they hit around 140 targets in Iran. That includes missile and drone sites, naval capabilities.
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah serangan putaran ketiga AS terhadap Iran yang kita lihat dalam beberapa hari terakhir. Tentu saja, ini terjadi setelah rezim Iran mengonfirmasi akan menutup kembali Selat Hormuz, jalur pelayaran kritis ini. Dan kami juga mendapat informasi lebih lanjut dari militer AS. Komando Pusat AS mengatakan mereka menyerang sekitar 140 target di Iran, termasuk pangkalan rudal dan drone, fasilitas angkatan laut.

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: That also includes ammunition storage facilities in Iran, as well as communication networks and coastal surveillance locations. Now, of course, the Strait of Hormuz continues to be a key stumbling block in efforts to mediate some sort of peaceful resolution between the U.S. and Iran. Iran has accused vessels of traveling through the Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized route.
paragraphs.cn: Juga termasuk fasilitas penyimpanan amunisi Iran, serta jaringan komunikasi dan lokasi pengawasan pantai. Tentu saja, Selat Hormuz tetap menjadi hambatan kunci dalam upaya mediasi penyelesaian damai AS-Iran. Iran menuduh kapal-kapal melintasi Selat Hormuz melalui rute yang tidak sah.

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: So, not using that corridor outlined by the Iranian regime along its coastal front, but rather using a corridor closer to the coast of Oman. But, of course, this also comes as Iran and Oman carry out bilateral talks. We saw on Saturday Iran's foreign minister meeting with his Omani counterpart, Bashar al-Assad.
paragraphs.cn: Artinya, tidak menggunakan koridor yang ditetapkan rezim Iran di garis pantainya, melainkan menggunakan koridor di dekat pantai Oman. Tapi tentu saja, ini juga terjadi saat Iran dan Oman mengadakan pembicaraan bilateral. Kami melihat Menteri Luar Negeri Iran bertemu dengan Menteri Luar Negeri Oman Bashar al-Assad pada hari Sabtu.

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: where, according to sources, discussions were had focusing on trying to establish some sort of equal control mechanism over the Strait of Hormuz to allow for two separate passage routes, including, of course, the Iranian corridor, but also through the coast of Oman as well. It's unclear at this stage whether there has been any firm resolution from those talks or whether that will be supported and championed by allies and neighbors in the Gulf region and, of course, the United States as well.
paragraphs.cn: Menurut sumber, pembicaraan berfokus pada upaya membangun semacam mekanisme kontrol yang setara atas Selat Hormuz untuk memungkinkan dua rute pelayaran independen, termasuk koridor Iran, tetapi juga pantai Oman. Belum jelas apakah pembicaraan ini menghasilkan solusi pasti, atau apakah akan mendapat dukungan dan advokasi dari sekutu dan tetangga Teluk serta AS.

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: And what we have seen in response to U.S. attacks on Iran just in the last 24 hours is, of course, retaliatory strikes by the IRGC as well, targeting U.S. allies in the Gulf, targeting military assets in the Gulf. We've heard from some of those Gulf nations saying that interceptions were carried out in the early hours of Sunday morning, that there were detected threats from Iran as well. And there are real fears that this could certainly destabilize those efforts to restart mediation efforts.
paragraphs.cn: Dan dalam 24 jam terakhir, sebagai tanggapan atas serangan AS terhadap Iran, kami melihat Garda Revolusi Islam juga melakukan serangan balasan, menargetkan sekutu dan aset militer AS di kawasan Teluk. Kami mendengar beberapa negara Teluk mengatakan bahwa pada Minggu dini hari dilakukan intersepsi dan mendeteksi ancaman dari Iran. Ada kekhawatiran nyata bahwa ini bisa merusak upaya untuk memulai kembali upaya mediasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Nada Bashir
paragraphs.en: We know that Iranian officials have made contact with officials in Pakistan, again, a key mediator in that initial memorandum of understanding. But at this stage, we are hearing very stark rhetoric from both the U.S., the Trump administration and the Iranian regime. And there are fears that this could certainly destabilize those efforts to mediate a renewed peace agreement.
paragraphs.cn: Kami tahu pejabat Iran telah melakukan kontak dengan pejabat Pakistan, yang merupakan mediator kunci dari nota kesepahaman awal itu. Tapi saat ini, kami mendengar retorika yang sangat keras dari pemerintahan Trump AS dan rezim Iran. Ada kekhawatiran bahwa ini bisa merusak upaya mediasi untuk kesepakatan damai baru.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Nada Bashir, thank you for that. And after President Trump declared that ceasefire agreement with Iran to be over, global oil prices started rising again. I want to go to Tom Kloza. He's the chief energy advisor for Gulf Oil. Tom, you have been in this space for decades now, and I'm interested in your perspective. I mean, Americans are seeing gas prices tick up.
paragraphs.cn: Nada Bashir, terima kasih atas laporannya. Setelah Presiden Trump mengumumkan berakhirnya gencatan senjata dengan Iran, harga minyak global mulai naik lagi. Saya ingin menghubungi Tom Kloza, kepala penasihat energi di Oil Price Information Service. Tom, Anda sudah puluhan tahun di bidang ini, saya tertarik dengan pandangan Anda. Maksud saya, orang Amerika melihat harga bensin naik.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: How much of this is directly tied to the Iran conflict and the war in Ukraine? And really, how much higher do you think that these gas prices could go in the near-term?
paragraphs.cn: Seberapa besar ini langsung terkait dengan konflik Iran dan perang Ukraina? Sebenarnya, menurut Anda seberapa besar harga bensin bisa naik dalam waktu dekat?

paragraphs.speaker: Tom Kloza
paragraphs.en: Well, I don't suspect we're going to see a spike in gas prices anytime soon, although we may go up back above four dollars here on the loss of some of the crude departures from the Persian Gulf. You know, we've got our eyes focused on the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf and the Iran war. But what's happening in the Ukraine- Russia war is really having more of an impact on U.S. gasoline prices. Overnight, another 5 percent of Russian refining was knocked out by Ukrainian drones.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya tidak berpikir kita akan melihat lonjakan harga bensin dalam waktu dekat, meskipun karena terganggunya ekspor minyak mentah dari sebagian Teluk Persia, harga bisa kembali ke atas $4 per galon. Anda tahu, kami terus memantau Selat Hormuz, Teluk Persia, dan perang Iran. Tapi perang Ukraina-Rusia sebenarnya memiliki dampak lebih besar pada harga bensin AS. Dalam semalam, drone Ukraina menghancurkan lagi 5% kapasitas penyulingan Rusia.

paragraphs.speaker: Tom Kloza
paragraphs.en: And, you know, that together with the loss of refining in the Persian Gulf, with the loss of refining or messy operations in South America has made it so that there's plenty of crude oil for the moment, but there's not plenty of gasoline and there's particularly not plenty of diesel fuel.
paragraphs.cn: Dan, Anda tahu, ditambah dengan hilangnya kapasitas penyulingan di Teluk Persia, serta hilangnya kapasitas penyulingan atau kekacauan operasi di Amerika Selatan, menyebabkan saat ini pasokan minyak mentah melimpah, tetapi pasokan bensin tidak mencukupi, terutama pasokan solar.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: And I hear you because you're what you're saying is it sounds like you're saying the Russia-Ukraine war and that conflict is the bigger driver of gas prices right now, despite our focus on the Strait of Hormuz. But I'm wondering, you know, when does that change? I mean, we don't really know when things will be resolved in the Strait of Hormuz. We know that Iran said that the Strait is closed and we're clearly not any closer to peace in that region.
paragraphs.cn: Saya mengerti maksud Anda, karena Anda mengatakan sepertinya perang dan konflik Rusia-Ukraina saat ini adalah pendorong utama harga bensin, meskipun kami fokus pada Selat Hormuz. Tapi saya ingin tahu, kapan situasi ini akan berubah? Maksud saya, kami benar-benar tidak tahu kapan masalah Selat Hormuz akan selesai. Kami tahu Iran mengatakan selat itu ditutup, dan kami jelas masih jauh dari perdamaian di kawasan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Is there a deadline for, you know, the uncertainty for the Strait of Hormuz over the next month or two, if that is not opened and a deal is not settled for safe passing for ships through the Strait of Hormuz? Could that then become a significant driver of gas prices? Again, I understand the Russia-Ukraine is the driver right now, but when might that change for the Strait?
paragraphs.cn: Apakah ada tenggat waktu untuk ketidakpastian Selat Hormuz dalam satu atau dua bulan ke depan? Jika selat tidak dibuka dan tidak ada kesepakatan untuk kelancaran lalu lintas kapal melalui Selat Hormuz, maka apakah ini akan menjadi pendorong utama harga bensin? Sekali lagi, saya pahami saat ini perang Rusia-Ukraina adalah pendorong utama, tapi kapan situasi Selat Hormuz bisa berubah?

paragraphs.speaker: Kloza
paragraphs.en: Well, the back half of the summer is going to be pretty critical to watch. We've got plenty of crude oil at the moment, not necessarily three months from now, is that there have been a lot of releases from the strategic petroleum reserves by the U.S. and by some foreign countries. Those run out in about 30 days. And that could mean that -- you know, and again, it's up against August's worldwide demand, when demand for all fuels is about as high as it's ever been.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, pengamatan akhir musim panas akan sangat kritis. Saat ini kami memiliki minyak mentah yang melimpah, tapi tiga bulan lagi belum tentu, karena AS dan beberapa negara asing telah melepaskan banyak minyak dari cadangan strategis mereka. Cadangan itu akan habis dalam waktu sekitar 30 hari. Ini bisa berarti -- Anda tahu, ini kembali berhadapan dengan puncak permintaan global pada bulan Agustus, saat permintaan semua bahan bakar mencapai level tertinggi sepanjang sejarah.

paragraphs.speaker: Kloza
paragraphs.en: So, we do have to worry about that later this summer. And we also have to worry about a hurricane season in August and September.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, kami benar-benar perlu khawatir tentang ini pada akhir musim panas nanti. Dan kami juga perlu khawatir tentang musim badai pada bulan Agustus dan September.

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes, I was going to ask you about that.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya baru saja hendak menanyakan itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Kloza
paragraphs.en: We cannot afford to lose any refined -- yes. We can't afford to lose any refined products manufacturing in the U.S. Gulf. And, you know, more than half of U.S. refined products comes from the Gulf of Mexico and usually on the coasts.
paragraphs.cn: 我们承受不起任何炼油 -- -- 是的。我们承受不起美国墨西哥湾任何成品油生产能力的损失。而且，你知道，美国超过一半的成品油来自墨西哥湾，通常来自沿海地区。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, to your point about hurricane season, I mean, it's -- we're mid-July now. I know people like you, you're thinking about a major hurricane and the Gulf hitting maybe refining capacity. Are we essentially one disruption away from more severe gas prices considering we've got these two unresolved conflicts going on that are already taxing the supply?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，关于你提到的飓风季节，现在已经是7月中旬了。我知道像你这样的人正在考虑一场大型飓风袭击墨西哥湾，可能影响炼油能力。考虑到我们有两个未解决的冲突已经在给供应带来压力，我们是否基本上离更严重的油价只有一次干扰的距离？

paragraphs.speaker: Kloza
paragraphs.en: Yes. We're always one disruption away. You know, on the West Coast of the United States, you're one refinery event away from $6 and $7 and $8 gasoline. And the rest of the world, we're a -- you know, an event or if, you know, it's hard to determine how much oil is leaving the Persian Gulf region because you've got propaganda on both sides. But we know it's nowhere near what it used to be. And we also know that it can be compromised.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们总是离一次干扰只有一步之遥。你知道，在美国西海岸，一次炼油厂事件就能让汽油价格达到每加仑6、7、8美元。而在世界其他地方，我们 -- -- 你知道，一次事件或者，嗯，很难确定有多少石油离开波斯湾地区，因为双方都在进行宣传。但我们知道远不如从前。而且我们也知道它可能受到威胁。

paragraphs.speaker: Kloza
paragraphs.en: I'm more worried about Iranian attacks on infrastructure in the region that could cripple some refineries and some logistics terminals, you know, down the road. For now, there's plenty of crude oil. There's not plenty of gasoline and there's --
paragraphs.cn: 我更担心伊朗对该地区基础设施的袭击，这可能会在未来削弱一些炼油厂和物流终端。目前，原油供应充足，但汽油并不充裕，而且 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Tom Kloza, thank you so much for joining us this morning. We're going to take a quick break. Then we will continue our breaking news coverage of the death of Senator Lindsey Graham at the age of 71. Stick with
paragraphs.cn: 汤姆·克洛扎，非常感谢您今天早上加入我们。我们稍作休息，然后继续报道参议员林赛·格雷厄姆去世的突发新闻，享年71岁。请继续关注。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn. Marsh
paragraphs.en: We return to our breaking news out of Washington. Longtime Republican senator, Lindsey Graham, has died. His office announced overnight that the senator died from a brief and sudden illness. 911 dispatch audio obtained by CNN reveals emergency personnel responded to a call for cardiac arrest at Graham's home in D.C. on Saturday evening. Graham had just celebrated his 71st birthday just last week. Now, with me now is CNN political analyst and Republican strategist, Brad Todd. Brad, good morning.
paragraphs.cn: 我们回到来自华盛顿的突发新闻。长期担任共和党参议员的林赛·格雷厄姆去世。他的办公室连夜宣布，参议员因突发短暂疾病去世。CNN获得的911调度音频显示，紧急救援人员于周六晚上响应了格雷厄姆在华盛顿家中因心脏骤停的呼叫。格雷厄姆上周刚庆祝了他的71岁生日。现在，与我连线的是CNN政治分析师兼共和党策略师布拉德·托德。布拉德，早上好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn. Marsh
paragraphs.en: Thank you so much for joining us on this breaking news day.
paragraphs.cn: 非常感谢您在这个突发新闻日加入我们。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Such sad news, shocking news. I know that you have known Senator Graham for a number of years. You first met him back in 1995. Tell me about that relationship and what you will remember most about him.
paragraphs.cn: 真是令人悲伤和震惊的消息。我知道您认识格雷厄姆参议员多年。你们第一次见面是在1995年。请谈谈你们的关系以及您对他最深刻的记忆。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, I was a press secretary on Capitol Hill in 1995 when Senator Graham was first elected to the House. And, you know, that was the Republican takeover. Republicans hadn't held a chamber, held the U.S. House in 40 years. So, he was part of that that class that took over. I was working on the Hill for another freshman member of Congress who became kind of an ally and a comrade of Senator Graham's.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。1995年，当格雷厄姆参议员首次当选众议员时，我是国会山的一名新闻秘书。你知道，那是共和党夺回众议院的时候。共和党已经40年没有控制过众议院了。所以，他是接管众议院的那一届议员中的一员。我当时在国会山为另一位新当选的众议员工作，他后来成为了格雷厄姆参议员的盟友和同志。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: And, you know, there was a conservative band of -- I think they would probably have called themselves guerrillas or rogues in that time who were trying to pull Speaker Newt Gingrich a little bit further in the conservative direction. And he was part of that of that cabal, if you will. And so, I got to know him then in his very early days as a House member.
paragraphs.cn: 而且，当时有一个保守派团体 -- -- 我想他们可能自称为游击队或叛逆者 -- -- 试图将议长纽特·金里奇进一步推向保守方向。格雷厄姆就是那个小团体的一员。所以，我在他担任众议员的早期就认识了他。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: And then, of course, have -- you know, having worked in Republican campaigns and especially on the Senate side over the last 20 years, I've been doing this for 30 something years, but I've done a lot of Senate races. And so, you, of course, interact with Senator Graham. And the reason is, is Senator Graham is a 24-hour senator. He just never stops doing the job or never stopped doing the job. And that's that he almost did the work of three people because of that.
paragraphs.cn: 然后，当然，在过去20年里，我参与共和党竞选活动，尤其是参议院方面的竞选，我已经从事这项工作30多年了，但参与了很多参议院竞选。因此，你自然会与格雷厄姆参议员互动。原因是，格雷厄姆参议员是一个全天候的参议员。他从不停止工作，或者说从未停止过工作。正因为如此，他几乎做了三个人的工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, speaking of his impact, I mean, what do you see as his greatest impact on the Republican Party?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。说到他的影响，您认为他对共和党最大的影响是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: Well, you know, first off, he's a compromiser, and I say that in a positive way. I mean, the Senate is fundamentally set up to accomplish anything only with a lot of compromise and work across the aisle. That's pretty hard to come by these days. But Senator Graham never quit trying to do it. Every meaningful effort to bring Republicans and Democrats together on a given issue. And of course, this happens a lot of times well outside the eye of cable news.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，首先，他是一个妥协者，我这么说是在积极意义上。我的意思是，参议院从根本上来说，只有通过大量的妥协和跨党派合作才能完成任何事情。这在当今很难实现。但格雷厄姆参议员从未放弃尝试。他参与了每一项有意义的努力，将共和党人和民主党人聚集在一起讨论某个议题。当然，这些很多时候都在有线新闻的视线之外进行。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: There are things that pass Congress that are not controversial in the end. Well, the reason they're not controversial in the end is because people like Lindsey Graham did the hard work in the back rooms ahead of time to make them pass. You know, that's just the nature of how he was. He was open to learning and being an expert on a lot of different areas. You know, he was -- everyone's talked this morning on CNN here about his foreign policy impact.
paragraphs.cn: 有些法案在国会通过时最终并不具争议性。而它们最终不具争议的原因，是因为像林赛·格雷厄姆这样的人提前在幕后做了艰苦的工作，使它们得以通过。你知道，这就是他的本性。他乐于学习，并在许多不同领域成为专家。你知道，他 -- -- 今天早上CNN这里每个人都在谈论他的外交政策影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: And certainly, you know, Israel and Ukraine have had no better friend than Senator Lindsey Graham. But he also was involved on a lot of other issues, that's vetting judicial nominees and working on thorny privacy issues. He chaired the Judiciary Committee, which is part of the intellectual property issues come to be settled in this Internet age. Well, Senator Graham was deeply involved in that and in the middle of those things.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，你知道，以色列和乌克兰没有比参议员林赛·格雷厄姆更好的朋友了。但他也参与了许多其他问题，比如审查司法提名和处理棘手的隐私问题。他曾担任司法委员会主席，该委员会负责解决互联网时代的知识产权问题。参议员格雷厄姆深度参与其中，并处于这些事务的核心。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: And all the matters about our legal system and the complicated matters like antitrust measures that come up, he was deep involved in those too. They didn't make the headlines or Twitter, but he was a man who was pretty broad and pretty deep in his policy expertise.
paragraphs.cn: 所有关于我们法律体系的事务，以及像反垄断措施这样复杂的问题，他也深度参与其中。这些没有成为头条新闻或推特热点，但他是一个政策专业知识相当广泛且深入的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes, I mean, we heard the president say he was always working. You said 24-hour senator.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我的意思是，我们听到总统说他一直在工作。你说他是24小时参议员。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Yes. And then you talk about his foreign policy and his experience and also his effectiveness. I just wonder, with someone like that gone and as a Republican yourself, I mean, how do you feel the void will be filled? Is there anyone that fills that void?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。然后你谈到他的外交政策、他的经验以及他的效率。我只是想知道，像这样的人离开了，而你自己也是共和党人，我的意思是，你觉得这个空缺将如何填补？有人能填补这个空缺吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: You can't fill what he did. You can't do it because no one works 24 hours a day. I mean, the American government was Lindsey's spouse and child. It was his hobby. It was his passion. It was his advocation. So, if you as an individual could think of how you react to your spouse and your child and your hobby, then roll all that up with your job. That's what it is. You can't replace him. Others will take the reins.
paragraphs.cn: 你无法填补他所做的一切。你做不到，因为没有人一天工作24小时。我的意思是，美国政府就是林赛的配偶和孩子。那是他的爱好。那是他的激情。那是他的职业。所以，如果你作为个人，能想象你如何对待你的配偶、孩子和爱好，然后把所有这些与你的工作结合起来。就是这样。你无法取代他。其他人会接手。

paragraphs.speaker: Todd
paragraphs.en: Tom Cotton will become the voice on foreign policy affairs, I think, and Dave McCormick that Lindsey filled those two. But you can't replace him.
paragraphs.cn: 汤姆·科顿将成为外交政策事务的代言人，我认为，还有戴夫·麦考密克，林赛填补了这两个位置。但你无法取代他。

paragraphs.speaker: Marsh
paragraphs.en: Thank you so much, Brad Todd. Thank you for joining us. And thank you for joining us for CNN This Morning. Inside Politics Sunday with Manu Raju is up next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 非常感谢你，布拉德·托德。感谢你加入我们。也感谢你收看CNN今晨节目。接下来是《曼努·拉朱的周日内部政治》。var CNN = CNN || {};

sentences.en: His office announced this morning that the longtime senator died from, quote, "a brief and sudden illness," but did not give further details.
sentences.cn: Kantor hari ini mengumumkan bahwa senator senior tersebut meninggal karena, kutipan, 'penyakit singkat dan mendadak', tetapi tidak memberikan rincian lebih lanjut.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (His office announced) + klausa objek (that...) + sisipan (quote...) + konjungsi kontras (but did not give...)
sentences.analysis: quote menunjukkan kutipan langsung, sering digunakan dalam berita; 'a brief and sudden illness' adalah ekspresi ringkas berita, mendeskripsikan penyebab kematian secara singkat; but menghubungkan kontras, menunjukkan informasi terbatas.

sentences.en: President Trump paid tribute to Graham in a post on Truth Social, saying, quote, "Senator Lindsey Graham, one of the greatest people and senators I have ever known is dead."
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统在 Truth Social 上发帖悼念格雷厄姆，引用原话："参议员林赛·格雷厄姆，我所认识的最伟大的人和参议员之一，去世了。"
sentences.structure: 主句（President Trump paid tribute）+ 状语（in a post...）+ 伴随状语（saying...）+ 直接引语（quote...）
sentences.analysis: paid tribute to 是固定搭配，表示"向...致敬"；one of the greatest... 是同位语结构，修饰 Lindsey Graham；quote 再次用于直接引语。

sentences.en: What we can tell you from dispatch audio from emergency response was that there was a piece of dispatch audio that came in that was heard at about 8:30 p.m. eastern time on Saturday evening.
sentences.cn: 我们能从应急响应调度音频中告诉你的是，有一段调度音频在周六晚上东部时间大约8点30分被听到。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What we can tell you...）+ 系动词（was）+ 表语从句（that there was...）+ 定语从句（that came in...that was heard...）
sentences.analysis: 主语从句 What we can tell you... 作主语；表语从句 that there was...；定语从句 that came in 和 that was heard 修饰 audio；时间状语 at about 8:30 p.m. eastern time。

sentences.en: We cannot confirm at this moment that the senator died of cardiac arrest.
sentences.cn: 我们目前无法确认这位参议员死于心脏骤停。
sentences.structure: 主句（We cannot confirm）+ 宾语从句（that...）
sentences.analysis: confirm that... 是常见结构；died of 表示"死于"；cardiac arrest 是医学术语；at this moment 表示"目前"。

sentences.en: That dispatch call discussed cardiac arrest, but of course, you know, those dispatch calls discuss various conditions that sometimes don't turn out to be the real cause of death or the cause of an injury.
sentences.cn: 那个调度电话讨论了心脏骤停，但当然，你知道，那些调度电话讨论的各种情况有时并非真正的死因或伤因。
sentences.structure: 主句（That dispatch call discussed...）+ 并列转折（but...）+ 插入语（of course, you know）+ 定语从句（that sometimes don't turn out...）
sentences.analysis: turn out to be 表示"结果是"；the real cause of death 是固定表达；插入语 of course, you know 体现口语化。

sentences.en: We also can piece together some detail elsewhere in an audio call, a dispatch audio, the discussion of someone suffering from chest pains.
sentences.cn: 我们还可以从另一段音频通话中拼凑出一些细节，即一段调度音频，讨论某人胸痛。
sentences.structure: 主句（We also can piece together...）+ 同位语（a dispatch audio）+ 同位语（the discussion...）
sentences.analysis: piece together 表示"拼凑"；suffering from 表示"遭受"；chest pains 是常见症状；同位语结构补充说明。

sentences.en: South Carolina's Republican governor, Henry McMaster, he is empowered to appoint a temporary replacement for Senator Graham.
sentences.cn: 南卡罗来纳州共和党州长亨利·麦克马斯特有权任命格雷厄姆参议员的临时接替者。
sentences.structure: 主语（South Carolina's Republican governor）+ 同位语（Henry McMaster）+ 主句（he is empowered to appoint...）
sentences.analysis: be empowered to do 表示"被授权做"；temporary replacement 是"临时接替者"；同位语结构提供身份信息。

sentences.en: Senator McMaster did issue this statement posted on X, quote, "Peggy and I and our children are devastated."
sentences.cn: 麦克马斯特参议员确实发布了这份声明，贴在X上，引用原话："佩吉、我和我们的孩子们都崩溃了。"
sentences.structure: 主句（Senator McMaster did issue...）+ 过去分词短语（posted on X）+ 直接引语（quote...）
sentences.analysis: did 强调谓语动词；issue a statement 是固定搭配；posted on X 是过去分词作定语；quote 引导直接引语。

sentences.en: That mention of Darline in that post from Governor McMaster is a reference to Senator Graham's younger sister, Darline, who Senator Graham actually helped raise when their parents died, when both he and his younger sister were very young.
sentences.cn: 麦克马斯特州长那篇帖子中提到的达琳指的是格雷厄姆参议员的妹妹达琳，格雷厄姆参议员在他们的父母去世时实际上帮助抚养了她，当时他和他的妹妹都非常年幼。
sentences.structure: 主句（That mention...is a reference...）+ 同位语（Darline）+ 定语从句（who Senator Graham helped raise...）+ 时间状语从句（when their parents died）+ 时间状语从句（when both...were very young）
sentences.analysis: a reference to 表示"提及"；help raise 是"帮助抚养"；when 引导两个时间状语从句，说明背景。

sentences.en: Senator Graham was an undergraduate in college, his sister, Darline, was a teenager, he helped raise her and eventually adopted her.
sentences.cn: 格雷厄姆参议员当时是大学本科生，他的妹妹达琳是青少年，他帮助抚养她并最终收养了她。
sentences.structure: 并列句（Senator Graham was... + his sister...was... + he helped raise...and adopted...）
sentences.analysis: 三个并列分句描述事实；undergraduate 是"本科生"；teenager 是"青少年"；adopt 是"收养"。

sentences.en: Senate Majority Leader John Thune with a long statement posted on X, talking about how just dedicated, loyal and what a great friend that Senator Graham was and what just a powerful and influential senator he was.
sentences.cn: 参议院多数党领袖约翰·图恩在X上发布了一份长篇声明，谈到格雷厄姆参议员是多么敬业、忠诚，是一位多么伟大的朋友，以及他是一位多么强大且有影响力的参议员。
sentences.structure: 主句（Senate Majority Leader John Thune...）+ 伴随状语（talking about...）+ 宾语从句（how...and what...）
sentences.analysis: with a long statement 是伴随状语；talking about 后接两个并列的感叹句（how...和 what...），表示强调。

sentences.en: The senator's statement goes on for a while there just talking about, you know, how revered Senator Lindsey Graham was in the U.S. Senate.
sentences.cn: 参议员的声明继续了一段时间，只是谈论，你知道，林赛·格雷厄姆参议员在美国参议院中是多么受尊敬。
sentences.structure: 主句（The senator's statement goes on...）+ 伴随状语（talking about...）+ 插入语（you know）+ 宾语从句（how revered...）
sentences.analysis: goes on 表示"继续"；revered 是"受尊敬的"；插入语 you know 体现口语化。

sentences.en: We do know that Senator Graham was in Ukraine just a couple of days ago, meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy returning, we believe, he spoke to reporters actually on Friday from Ukraine and returning to the U.S. shortly after that the senator passing away in Washington, D.C.
sentences.cn: 我们确实知道格雷厄姆参议员几天前在乌克兰，与乌克兰总统泽连斯基会面，我们相信，他实际上在周五从乌克兰对记者发表了讲话，之后不久返回美国，参议员在华盛顿特区去世。
sentences.structure: 主句（We do know）+ 宾语从句（that...）+ 伴随状语（meeting with...）+ 插入语（we believe）+ 并列谓语（spoke...and returning...）+ 时间状语（shortly after that...）
sentences.analysis: do 强调；a couple of days ago 是"几天前"；meeting with 是伴随状语；we believe 是插入语；passing away 是"去世"。

sentences.en: He was someone who regularly spoke out on news of the day, he was, obviously, a fierce defender of Donald Trump and was a key ally of the White House on Capitol Hill.
sentences.cn: 他是一个经常就当日新闻发表言论的人，显然，他是唐纳德·特朗普的坚定捍卫者，也是白宫在国会山的关键盟友。
sentences.structure: 主句（He was someone）+ 定语从句（who regularly spoke out...）+ 并列句（he was...and was...）
sentences.analysis: spoke out on 表示"就...发表言论"；a fierce defender 是"坚定的捍卫者"；a key ally 是"关键盟友"；插入语 obviously。

sentences.en: He was part of the bipartisan Gang of Eight, a group of lawmakers who worked together to bolster border security, as well as create a path to legalization for millions of immigrants who were living in the shadows in this country.
sentences.cn: 他是两党"八人帮"的一员，这是一个由立法者组成的团体，他们共同努力加强边境安全，并为数百万生活在这个国家阴影中的移民创造合法化途径。
sentences.structure: 主句（He was part of...）+ 同位语（a group of lawmakers）+ 定语从句（who worked together...）+ 并列不定式（to bolster...as well as create...）+ 定语从句（who were living...）
sentences.analysis: bipartisan 是"两党的"；Gang of Eight 是专有名词；bolster 是"加强"；a path to legalization 是"合法化途径"；living in the shadows 是比喻，表示"生活在阴影中"。

sentences.en: He had worked really closely with his friend and longtime mentor, Senator John McCain, on that legislation.
sentences.cn: 他曾与他的朋友和长期导师约翰·麦凯恩参议员在那项立法上密切合作。
sentences.structure: 主句（He had worked...）+ 状语（with...）+ 同位语（his friend and longtime mentor, Senator John McCain）
sentences.analysis: work closely with 是"与...密切合作"；longtime mentor 是"长期导师"；同位语结构提供身份。

sentences.en: There were several instances, even once Donald Trump took office, that Senator Lindsey Graham became a major commentator and advocate on immigration related issues.
sentences.cn: 有几个例子，甚至在唐纳德·特朗普上任后，林赛·格雷厄姆参议员成为移民相关问题的主要评论员和倡导者。
sentences.structure: 主句（There were several instances）+ 时间状语从句（even once Donald Trump took office）+ 定语从句（that Senator...became...）
sentences.analysis: instances 是"例子"；took office 是"上任"；commentator 是"评论员"；advocate 是"倡导者"；immigration related issues 是"移民相关问题"。

sentences.en: Over time, his view and his position sort of evolved as the Republican Party's and President Trump's position really hardened the position of Republicans on that issue.
sentences.cn: 随着时间的推移，他的观点和立场有所演变，因为共和党和特朗普总统的立场确实使共和党人在该问题上的立场变得强硬。
sentences.structure: 主句（his view and his position evolved）+ 方式状语（sort of）+ 原因状语从句（as...hardened...）
sentences.analysis: over time 是"随着时间的推移"；sort of 是口语化表达，表示"有点"；evolve 是"演变"；harden 是"使强硬"。

sentences.en: He built his career as a defense hawk, as you noted, and as Brian noted, he was just in Ukraine a couple of days ago, meeting with the president of that country, Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
sentences.cn: 正如你所指出的，他建立了作为国防鹰派的职业生涯，正如布莱恩所指出的，他几天前就在乌克兰，与该国总统泽连斯基会面。
sentences.structure: 主句（He built his career...）+ 插入语（as you noted）+ 并列句（and as Brian noted, he was...meeting with...）
sentences.analysis: defense hawk 是"国防鹰派"；as you noted 和 as Brian noted 是插入语；meeting with 是伴随状语。

sentences.en: He has been a really strong ally of Ukraine in terms of suppl
sentences.cn: 他一直是乌克兰非常坚定的盟友，在供应方面...
sentences.structure: 主句（He has been a really strong ally...）+ 状语（in terms of suppl）
sentences.analysis: a strong ally 是"坚定的盟友"；in terms of 是"在...方面"；suppl 可能是 supply 的缩写，但原文不完整。

topics.title: 林赛·格雷厄姆参议员生平
topics.content: 林赛·格雷厄姆（Lindsey Graham）是美国共和党参议员，来自南卡罗来纳州。1994年当选众议员，2002年进入参议院，以国防鹰派和移民改革立场著称。他曾是2013年两党移民改革'八人帮'成员，也是特朗普的坚定盟友。格雷厄姆多次访问乌克兰，支持其对抗俄罗斯。他于2026年7月12日因突发疾病去世，享年71岁。
topics.keywords: Lindsey Graham · Gang of Eight · defense hawk · immigration reform

topics.title: 美国对伊朗军事打击
topics.content: 2026年7月12日，美国宣布对伊朗140个军事目标实施打击，标志着敌对行动恢复。此举可能加剧中东紧张局势。伊朗方面尚未回应。此前美伊关系因核问题等持续紧张。
topics.keywords: hostilities resume · military targets · Iran · U.S. military strike

topics.title: 新奥尔良洪水灾害
topics.content: 新奥尔良因暴雨引发严重洪水，城市部分地区被淹。当地政府已启动应急响应。新奥尔良地处低洼，易受洪水侵袭，2005年卡特里娜飓风曾造成灾难性破坏。
topics.keywords: New Orleans · floods · heavy rains · disaster response

topics.title: 美国参议员临时继任程序
topics.content: 当美国参议员去世或辞职时，州长有权任命临时继任者，直至特别选举。南卡罗来纳州州长亨利·麦克马斯特（Henry McMaster）将任命格雷厄姆的临时接替者。该任命需符合州法律，通常来自同一政党。
topics.keywords: temporary replacement · governor · appointment · Henry McMaster

quiz.question: 在原文 'He was always working and was a true American patriot.' 中，'patriot' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: patriot 意为爱国者，指热爱并支持自己国家的人。原文中特朗普称赞格雷厄姆是真正的美国爱国者。

quiz.question: 原文 'South Carolina's Republican governor, Henry McMaster, he is empowered to appoint a temporary replacement' 中，'empowered' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: empower 意为授权、赋予权力。这里指州长有权任命临时接替者。

quiz.question: 原文 'He built his career as a defense hawk' 中，'defense hawk' 指的是？
options: B. 主张强硬军事政策的人
quiz.explanation: defense hawk 指国防鹰派，即主张强大军事力量和强硬国防政策的人。

quiz.question: 根据报道，林赛·格雷厄姆参议员去世的可能原因是什么？
options: D. 心脏病发作（未确认）
quiz.explanation: 报道称其死于'brief and sudden illness'（短暂突发疾病），但未给出具体原因。调度录音提到心脏骤停，但未确认。

quiz.question: 谁将负责任命格雷厄姆的临时继任者？
options: B. 南卡罗来纳州州长
C. 参议院多数党领袖
D. 南卡罗来纳州议会
quiz.explanation: 报道明确指出南卡罗来纳州州长亨利·麦克马斯特有权任命临时接替者。

quiz.question: 格雷厄姆参议员在移民改革方面曾参与哪个两党小组？
quiz.explanation: 报道提到他是2013年两党移民改革'Gang of Eight'（八人帮）的成员。
